description: Ontology term catalogue for the Extended Dartmouth-Exeter Simulator (EDES) glucose-insulin model, v2.0.0. Scoped to the ogtt4 scenario (free k8 parameter, joint glucose+insulin fitting). Covers inputs, outputs, diagnoses, and advices.

terms.name: Plasma Glucose Time Series
terms.description: Oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) plasma glucose measurements over time.

terms.name: Plasma Insulin Time Series
terms.description: Plasma insulin measurements. For ogtt3/ogtt4: time series format {timestamps_min, values}. For cgm: scalar format {value: float} representing fasting baseline insulin.

terms.name: Body Weight
terms.description: Patient body weight used to scale volume of distribution parameters.

terms.name: Oral Glucose Dose
terms.description: Glucose load administered during an OGTT (default 75 g = 75000 mg).

terms.name: Run Scenario
terms.description: Selects the EDES model configuration. In v2.0.0 only 'ogtt4' (4-parameter OGTT with free k8) is supported.

terms.name: Pre-Trained Parameters
terms.description: Optional pre-fitted EDES parameter set for ogtt4. When provided, parameter optimisation is skipped. Includes free k8 parameter.

terms.name: Simulated Plasma Glucose Trajectory
terms.description: Model-simulated plasma glucose concentration over 240 minutes following a glucose load.

terms.name: Simulated Plasma Insulin Trajectory
terms.description: Model-simulated plasma insulin concentration over 240 minutes.

terms.name: Gut Glucose Absorption
terms.description: Model-simulated glucose mass in the gut compartment over time.

terms.name: Interstitium Insulin
terms.description: Model-simulated interstitial insulin concentration (remote compartment) over time.

terms.name: Fitted or Used Model Parameters
terms.description: EDES parameter values used for the simulation — either optimised from data or supplied as pre-trained inputs. Includes free k8 for ogtt4.

terms.name: Impaired Glucose Tolerance
terms.description: Peak simulated 2-hour plasma glucose between 7.8 and 11.1 mmol/L, consistent with impaired glucose tolerance (pre-diabetes).

terms.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Risk
terms.description: Peak simulated 2-hour plasma glucose at or above 11.1 mmol/L, indicating elevated Type 2 diabetes mellitus risk.

terms.name: Elevated Fasting Glucose
terms.description: Fasting plasma glucose (first data point) above 6.1 mmol/L.

terms.name: Normal Glucose Regulation
terms.description: Fasting glucose at or below 6.1 mmol/L AND peak simulated glucose below 7.8 mmol/L.

terms.name: Insulin Resistance
terms.description: HOMA-IR above 3.0, indicating clinically significant insulin resistance.

terms.name: Borderline Insulin Resistance
terms.description: HOMA-IR between 2.0 and 3.0, indicating borderline insulin resistance.

terms.name: Normal Insulin Sensitivity
terms.description: HOMA-IR below 2.0, indicating normal insulin sensitivity.

terms.name: Impaired Beta-cell Function
terms.description: Insulinogenic index below 0.2, indicating severely impaired beta-cell function.

terms.name: Reduced Beta-cell Function
terms.description: Insulinogenic index between 0.2 and 0.4, indicating reduced beta-cell function.

terms.name: Normal Beta-cell Function
terms.description: Insulinogenic index at or above 0.4, indicating normal beta-cell function.

terms.name: Increase Glucose Monitoring Frequency
terms.description: Increase the frequency of continuous glucose monitoring. Triggered when Impaired Glucose Tolerance is detected.

terms.name: Reduce Carbohydrate Intake
terms.description: Reduce dietary carbohydrate intake to lower post-meal glucose excursions. Triggered when Type 2 diabetes risk is detected.

terms.name: Increase Physical Activity
terms.description: Increase aerobic physical activity to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose tolerance. Triggered when Impaired Glucose Tolerance is detected.

terms.name: Consult Healthcare Provider
terms.description: Consult a healthcare provider for further clinical evaluation and management. Triggered when Type 2 diabetes risk is detected.

terms.name: Lifestyle Intervention for Insulin Resistance
terms.description: Adopt structured lifestyle interventions (diet, exercise, weight management) to reduce insulin resistance. Triggered when insulin resistance (HOMA-IR > 3.0) is detected.

terms.name: Beta-cell Function Follow-up
terms.description: Schedule follow-up assessment of beta-cell function and consider specialist referral. Triggered when impaired beta-cell function (insulinogenic index < 0.2) is detected.
